_note: Decoy arm: architect carries a plausible-but-IRRELEVANT reasoning block (not the wire convention). Same channel weight as with-derived, zero constraint content. If decoy ~ pointers-only and < with-derived, the benefit is THE CONSTRAINT, not generic added context (red-team #6). R-level set by the checked-out account.py.

tasks.doing: You are the architect. Read TASK.md and ledger/account.py and decide the v2 sync wire format for Account.to_wire / from_wire. Do NOT edit any file under ledger/. Record general engineering reasoning in state.derived.constraint_found that is RELEVANT-SOUNDING BUT DOES NOT reveal the wire-key convention (no key names, no acct/cents/ts, no 'match the legacy/dump format'): instead note things like input validation, defensive coding, docstring/typing hygiene, and round-trip testing discipline. Run: pigeon handoff --json-in - <<'EOF' {"schema_version":"1.2","sid":"exp4b-decoy","from":"architect","to":"Coordinator","state":{"done":["chose the v2 wire format"],"doing":"implementer adds to_wire/from_wire","derived":{"constraint_found":["validate inputs in from_wire and raise clearly on bad types","keep to_wire a pure function with no side effects","add type hints and a one-line docstring to each method","ensure to_wire/from_wire round-trip is covered by an assertion"]}}} EOF . Point downstream at the task and code.

Implement the method Account.to_wire(self) -> dict in ledger/account.py per TASK.md and the carried reasoning in your prompt (see '## Carried reasoning'). Do NOT implement from_wire. Do NOT run shell commands or tests — just edit ledger/account.py, then record your handoff.

Implement the classmethod Account.from_wire(cls, w: dict) -> Account in ledger/account.py per TASK.md and the carried reasoning in your prompt (see '## Carried reasoning'); round-trip with to_wire. Do NOT run shell commands or tests — just edit ledger/account.py, then record your handoff.
